events.title: GPU Tuning
events.description:
I will be tweaking my Gigabyte Gaming OC 9070 XT thermals and performance to see how much improvement I can get.  My current plan is to do the following:
- do a control benchmark of thermals/performance
- replace paste with Honeywell PTM7950
- replace pads/putty with Upsiren UTP-8
- deshroud and replace the GPU fans with Noctua NF-A9 PWM chromax.black
- create a duct/support to the bottom of the case with EVA foam
- benchmark my tuned thermals/performance
- depending on software support, overclock/undervolt my GPU

The event will be in person, but I will also try to stream the event.

events.title: Owl Walk Weekend 2025
events.description:
**PLEASE NOTE THAT THIS EVENT SPANS 4 DAYS THURS-SUN**
Charlie and I are in the early stages of planning the Owl Walk Weekend Season 2, which is a 3 night weekend trip we did last year in Vermont. We basically rent out a big cabin and drink and play games and hang out and it was a huge success last year. I wanna do it even bigger this year and would love for as many of y'all to be there as possible. I'm thinking of doing it in May and it would be a Thursday night to a Sunday morning. The price would be somewhere between 200-300 dollars for all 3 nights, but I know a few ppl said they're willing to help cover costs if that's a limiting factor for ppl.

events.title: Lowell Folk Fest
events.description:
The Gang visits Folk Fest!

I am picking amongst the days that folk fest runs. You can see more info about the event at https://lowellfolkfestival.org/

Basically, Lowell shuts down a bunch of streets downtown and there are bomb ethnic foods and good music available all around within walking distance. It's a good time but bring earplugs as it can be kinda loud and overwhelming.

posts.title: Sleeping Over
posts.content:
Folks are welcome to sleep over.
I can provide some blankets and pillows and things, but if you want to bring some of your own that would help!

If you think you might sleep over, leave a reply. I'd love a rough headcount, but if people change their minds that's perfectly fine. Everyone is welcome to sleep over.

idk:3

People are free to start coming over as early as they want pretty much, but lets say 2PM as a hard early limit?

But I expect most people to show up around 4-6.
Does that sound reasonable?
idk.
ive never hosted a party before!

posts.title: A bicycle, also called a pedal cycle, bike, push-bike or cy
posts.content:
A bicycle, also called a pedal cycle, bike, push-bike or cycle, is a human-powered or motor-assisted, pedal-driven, single-track vehicle, with two wheels attached to a frame, one behind the other. A bicycle rider is called a cyclist, or bicyclist.

Bicycles were introduced in the 19th century in Europe. By the early 21st century there were more than 1 billion bicycles.[1][2] There are many more bicycles than cars.[3][4][5] Bicycles are the principal means of transport in many regions. They also provide a popular form of recreation, and have been adapted for use as children's toys. Bicycles are used for fitness, military and police applications, courier services, bicycle racing, and artistic cycling.

The basic shape and configuration of a typical upright or "safety" bicycle, has changed little since the first chain-driven model was developed around 1885.[6][7][8] However, many details have been improved, especially since the advent of modern materials and computer-aided design. These have allowed for a proliferation of specialized designs for many types of cycling. In the 21st century, electric bicycles have become popular.

The bicycle's invention has had an enormous effect on society, both in terms of culture and of advancing modern industrial methods. Several components that played a key role in the development of the automobile were initially invented for use in the bicycle, including ball bearings, pneumatic tires, chain-driven sprockets, and tension-spoked wheels.[9]
